trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Svezia exports machinery, vehicles, pharmaceuticals, and telecom equipment to Sudafrica; Sudafrica exports minerals, precious metals, chemicals, and automotive parts to Svezia. Swedish firms (Volvo, Ericsson, SKF, IKEA) have established operations in Sudafrica.

diplomatic.summary: Svezia and Sudafrica maintain constructive diplomatic relations. Svezia was an opponent of apartheid and has longstanding historical ties to the ANC. However, Sudafrica's BRICS alignment, its abstention on Russia-Ucraina UN votes, and its ICJ case against Israele create divergence from Swedish positions. Development cooperation through Sida supports some bilateral engagement.

military.summary: No significant military ties. Both countries maintain independent defence policies. Svezia focuses on NATO. Sudafrica maintains a non-aligned defence posture. Some historical Swedish support for African peacekeeping through UN frameworks.

financial.summary: Swedish pension funds hold Sudafrican equities and bonds as part of emerging market exposure. Swedish firms (Volvo, Ericsson) have significant Sudafrican operations. Sudafrican mining conglomerates have some Svezia-listed equity but limited direct FDI.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Svezia assists Sudafrica through development aid in renewable energy transitions. Swedish firms (Vestas, ABB) participate in Sudafrica's renewable energy procurement programmes. No significant direct fossil fuel trade.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific collaboration, particularly in environmental science, sustainable development, and health research. Sida-funded research partnerships with Sudafrican universities. Collaboration in HIV/AIDS research and climate adaptation science.
